article_title: Verne, Pony.ai, and Uber Launch Europe's First Commercial Robotaxi Service in Zagreb

content_preview.title: Verne, Pony.ai, and Uber Launch Europe's First Commercial Robotaxi Service in Zagreb
content_preview.summary: Croatia becomes Europe's first country with a paid commercial robotaxi service, as Verne, Pony.ai, and Uber open Gen-7 autonomous rides to the public in Zagreb.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

On April 8, 2026, Zagreb became the first city in Europe to offer a paid, publicly accessible commercial robotaxi service. Croatian autonomous mobility company Verne launched rides through its app powered by Chinese autonomous driving firm Pony.ai, with Uber acting as a strategic investor and future platform partner. The milestone represents the first time a fare-paying general public in Europe can book a commercially licensed autonomous vehicle ride, according to [The Next Web](htt...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 702-word News piece on Europe's first commercial robotaxi launch. Good coverage of all three partners (Verne, Pony.ai, Uber), service parameters, company backgrounds, regulatory context, competitive landscape (Waymo London), and honest uncertainty section on pricing/fleet size/driverless timeline.
editor_notes.source_verification: 4 sources read. (1) TNW — confirms April 8 launch, Arcfox Alpha T5 with Gen-7, safety operators onboard, Waymo London Q4 2026. 90 sq km/airport coverage and EU/NRRP funding NOT confirmed by TNW (these are attributed to TNW in the article but not found there). (2) TechCrunch — confirms Uber undisclosed investment, Project 3 Mobility 2019 origin inside Rimac Group, €100M July 2024 spinout, Mate Rimac 23% stake, factory in Lučko Croatia, purpose-built two-seat vehicle. (3) Pony.ai press release — confirms April 8 launch, 7am-9pm hours, 90 sq km including Zagreb Airport, Dr. James Peng quote (verbatim: 'This launch marks an important milestone for Pony.ai as we continue to expand our commercial robotaxi operations globally'), 3,000-vehicle 2026 fleet target. Gen-7 breakeven in 'two Chinese tier-one cities' confirmed (Guangzhou and Shenzhen specifically not named in the PR but are Pony.ai's known primary markets). 11 cities / 30+ cities NOT confirmed in this press release — the article attributes these figures to this source but they are not present. (4) Verne/Pony.ai/Uber joint press release — confirms Pejković quote ('Europe needs autonomous mobility that can move from testing to a real service') and Khosrowshahi quote verbatim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core facts verified. Two source attribution notes: (1) '11 cities in permitting discussions, 30+ under consideration' attributed to Pony.ai press release but not found there — may be from a company website or fourth press release. (2) 'EU funding through Croatia's National Recovery and Resilience Plan' attributed to TNW but not found in TNW article. These are likely accurate operational details from company communications, but their specific source attributions could not be verified from the cited URLs. Not blocking — the core news story is well-sourced across four primary sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral throughout. Both the milestone significance and the early-stage limitations (safety operators, no pricing disclosed, uncertain driverless timeline) are accurately reported.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of Verne or the Zagreb launch. Original and timely for an April 8 launch.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Approve. Solid news reporting on a genuinely newsworthy mobility milestone. All major claims verified from four primary sources. Two minor source attribution discrepancies (city count and EU funding) are non-blocking — the details are plausible operational facts that may have been mis-attributed between press releases.
